item: UMC internal customer overlap by country
related_strategy: Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines, Indonesia watchlists
why_it_matters: No non-Singapore option should move beyond Stage 0 without named customer relevance.
next_action: Map Fab 12i and UMC mature-node customers by SEA manufacturing, EMS, packaging, and support footprint.
decision_impact: Blocks Stage 1 for sales, engineering, packaging, and foundry options.

why_it_matters: A local presence only matters if it creates revenue, retention, cycle-time, yield, or support-cost benefit.
next_action: Define whether value comes from wafer pull, packaging attach, support fees, retention, or cost avoidance.

why_it_matters: Malaysia is only actionable if a named partner model exists with capacity, quality, IP, and commercial fit.
next_action: Shortlist OSAT, test, substrate, equipment, and service partners; screen conflicts and customer acceptance.
decision_impact: Required before Malaysia can move from conditional watchlist to Stage 1.

why_it_matters: Ecosystem depth does not prove margin, yield, cycle-time, or commercial benefit to UMC.
next_action: Request indicative pricing, yield responsibility, capacity commitments, and commercial terms.

why_it_matters: Without attach-rate, Malaysia OSAT adjacency cannot be converted into revenue or ROIC.
next_action: Estimate Fab 12i wafer output and customer packages that could realistically attach to Malaysia partners.

related_strategy: All new operating or investment options
why_it_matters: Management cannot compare watchlists without cost envelope and operating model.
next_action: Prepare light capex / opex ranges for partnership, office, support hub, and manufacturing scenarios.

why_it_matters: The dashboard should not imply investment readiness without hurdle-rate comparison.
next_action: Build preliminary ROIC and payback model using validated revenue, capex, opex, incentives, and utilization inputs.

why_it_matters: Policy statements do not equal project-specific cash grants, tax holidays, land, power, or permitting terms.
next_action: Request project-specific incentive menus and eligibility conditions by country and site.
decision_impact: Can change economics but does not override customer and ROIC gates.

why_it_matters: Tax leakage, transfer pricing, withholding, and incentive clawbacks affect ROIC.
next_action: Prepare country-level tax issue list for partnership, services, and capex scenarios.

why_it_matters: Power reliability and tariff structure affect manufacturing feasibility and support-site resilience.
next_action: Collect site-level electricity tariffs, outage history, redundancy options, and service-level commitments.
decision_impact: Blocks non-Singapore foundry expansion; informs support hub economics.

why_it_matters: Fab expansion requires validated power, water, environmental permitting, and redundancy.
next_action: Do not begin Stage 1 until site-level utility envelopes and permitting paths are documented.

why_it_matters: Generic engineer wages do not price FAE, process, packaging, quality, or product engineering roles.
next_action: Collect salary bands by function and seniority in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines, and Indonesia.
decision_impact: Required for support hub and partner economics.

why_it_matters: A country may have engineers but not the specific semiconductor functions UMC needs.
next_action: Validate availability for FAE, quality, test, packaging, process, equipment, product, and program roles.

why_it_matters: Entity setup, employment, customer data, export controls, incentives, and IP boundaries affect feasibility.
next_action: Prepare country-specific legal feasibility memo by option type.
decision_impact: Required before any operating launch or partner transaction.
